topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of aged paper and strong coffee hung heavy in the air of the study. I found the manuscript tucked beneath a stack of annotated scripts, the cover a simple, elegant title page: *The Unfolding*. My hands trembled slightly as I traced the embossed lettering. It was my life, meticulously chronicled, every shared glance, every muttered complaint, every quiet victory. A coldness seeped into my chest, a sudden, vast emptiness where familiarity used to reside. I reread a passage describing a mundane Tuesday afternoon, but the words felt alien, borrowed. I looked up, expecting to see him, but he wasn't there. The silence of the room amplified the frantic drumming in my ears.

[story 2]

It started with a misplaced bookmark, a thin strip of cream cardstock slipped into the margin of a chapter I’d been reading late one night. Curiosity, sharp and unwelcome, pulled me to the desk. What I found wasn't just notes; it was prose. His voice, the cadence of his thoughts, woven into sentences that described my habits with an unnerving intimacy. My breath hitched, catching somewhere in my throat. I sat back in my chair, the leather groaning beneath me, my vision blurring at the edges. Everything felt tilted, as if the ground beneath my feet had shifted, leaving me unsteady on my own skin.

[story 3]

I watched him across the dinner table, the light catching the silver in his hair. He laughed at something I said, a sound that used to anchor me, but now it felt distant, like listening to a recording played through thick glass. Later, alone in the living room, I found the journal—or rather, the novel. The descriptions were so precise, so utterly true, that a strange vertigo washed over me. Who was the author? Was this a performance? My fingers tightened around my wine glass, the crystal suddenly too sharp. I kept glancing toward the doorway, waiting for him to materialize, needing a solid point of reference in a world that suddenly seemed to be spinning too fast.
